title: Why higher-proof blanco tequila is a hot spirits trend
summary: 'The agave flavours hit you in waves...'
content: <p>Carissa Chesanek is a food and spirits writer based in Brooklyn. Her work has been seen in <em>Imbibe, InsideHook, Food Network, Vinepair, Whisky Advocate</em>, and<em> Alcohol Professor</em>. When she's not writing, she's on the hunt for the best Old Fashioned.</p>

title: Sam Neill: A tribute and recent interview with the late winemaker and actor
summary: Acclaimed actor and tireless advocate for New Zealand wine...
content: <div class="author-description"><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Julie Sheppard joined the Decanter team in 2018 and is Regional Editor for Australia, New Zealand and South Africa &amp; Spirits Editor. </span></p><p class="p1"><span class="s1">Before Decanter, she worked for a range of drinks and food titles, including as managing editor of both <em>Imbibe</em> and <em>Square Meal</em>, associate publisher of </span><em>The Drinks Business</em><span class="s1">, senior editor of the Octopus Publishing Group and Supplements editor of <em>Harpers Wine &amp; Spirit</em>. As a contributor, she has over 20 years’ experience writing <span class="Apple-converted-space">about food, drink and travel </span>for a wide range of publications, including <em><span class="Apple-converted-space">Condé Nast Traveller, </span></em></span><span class="s1"><em>Delicious</em>, <em>Waitrose Kitchen</em>, <em>Waitrose Drinks</em>, <em>Time Out</em> and national newspapers including <em>The Telegraph</em> and <em>The Sunday Times</em>.</span></p><p class="p1"><span class="s1">.</span></p></div>

title: DWWA 2026 Best in Show: The sweet and fortified wines that made it to the top

content: <div class="author-description"><p>Sophie Thorpe is a London-based wine writer, largely writing in-house for merchant Fine &amp; Rare. The winner of the 2021 Guild of Food Writers Drinks Writing Award and an MW student, her writing can be found at <a href="https://www.firstpress.uk/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">firstpress.uk</a>.</p></div>

title: Where are the world’s best bar and restaurant wine lists? Meet the winners for 2026
summary: Want to know where to find the ultimate by-the-glass or sparkling wine list near you? Or maybe you’re concerned about sustainability or obsessed with Italy? Star Wine List reveals this year’s best of the best from around the world.
content: <p>Tina Gellie has worked for <em>Decanter</em> since 2008 across a number of editorial roles and is currently the brand's Content Director. An awarded wine writer and editor, she won several scholarships on the way to getting her WSET Diploma, and is a freeman of The Worshipful Company of Distillers. She has worked in wine publishing since 2003, including as Deputy Editor and Acting Editor of <em>Wine International</em>. Before her wine career she was a newspaper journalist for broadsheets in London and Australia.</p>

title: Five French wines to celebrate Bastille Day
summary: The national day of France is celebrated on 14 July with parties, fireworks, parades and, of course, wine.
content: <p>Natalie is Decanter's France editor, commissioning and writing content on French wines (excluding Bordeaux) across print and digital. She writes Decanter's coverage of Languedoc wines, as well as a monthly magazine column, The Ethical Drinker, which unpicks the thorny topic of sustainability in wine. </p><p>It was during her time studying for a French and Italian degree that Natalie began her foray into wine: tutoring French in exchange for WSET lessons in her spare time (she now realises who got the better deal!). She moved to the Languedoc after graduating to work for a vineyard tour company, before returning to the UK in 2016 to join the tastings team at Decanter.</p><p>She worked across Decanter's panel tastings and wine competitions before becoming awards competition manager, overseeing the competitive and judging elements of the Decanter World Wine Awards, Decanter Asia Wine Awards and Retailer Awards, and completing her WSET Diploma in 2019.</p><p>In 2021 she made the shift to the Decanter editorial team, and is now the Regional Editor for France (outside of Bordeaux and Burgundy).</p><p>She will always be drawn to the wines of the Languedoc and Roussillon, but her wine tastes are wide-ranging and she can't resist a glass of Manzanilla Sherry or the lure of an obscure grape variety.</p>
